summary: First-iteration backend testing for PawGroom API. All 31 pytest cases passed (100%) covering public catalog, admin auth + services CRUD, admin bookings + stats, customer auth via seeded Mongo sessions, booking creation with GST math, ownership isolation, PDF invoice download, and edge cases (expired session, unsupported city, empty items, invalid status, unauthorized access).

minor.issue: Logout only deletes the session if the session_token cookie is present in the request. When the client sends only Authorization: Bearer <token>, the token is not read for deletion and the session persists in DB. Consider also reading the Bearer token as a fallback for API/mobile clients.
Missing input validation - accepts negative base_price and duration_minutes, and any arbitrary pet_type string (not restricted to dog/cat). Consider adding Pydantic validators.
No validation that slot_date is in the future or that slot_time matches an allowed slot from /api/slots. Also pincode/phone accept arbitrary strings.
@app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown') is deprecated in FastAPI; migrate to lifespan context. Non-blocking but will break on future FastAPI upgrade.

action_items: Optional: enhance /api/auth/logout to accept Bearer token in addition to cookie for full API-client support.
Optional: add Pydantic validators to ServiceCreate/BookingCreate (positive numbers, pet_type enum, pincode/phone regex).
Optional: migrate deprecated on_event startup/shutdown handlers to FastAPI lifespan.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~660 lines and mixes models, auth, catalog, admin, bookings and PDF generation. Consider splitting into modules (auth.py, admin.py, bookings.py, invoice.py, seed.py) if it grows further.

CORS is configured with allow_credentials=True but allow_origins defaults to '*' via env — the browser will reject credentialed requests when origin is '*'. In production, CORS_ORIGINS must list explicit origins.
Emergent OAuth exchange stores the returned session_token verbatim, but there is no cleanup of expired user_sessions. Add a TTL index on user_sessions.expires_at.
get_current_user handles both cookie and Bearer, but logout only handles cookie. Inconsistent between endpoints.
No rate limiting on /api/admin/login — susceptible to brute force. Consider throttling.
Payment status is set to 'paid' purely because a upi_txn_ref string is provided — no verification; acknowledged as MVP behavior but noteworthy for production.
test_credentials: Admin admin@pawgroom.in / Admin@123 (seeded). Customer sessions seeded in-test into MongoDB users/user_sessions.
seed_data_creation: Tests seed TEST_-prefixed users, sessions and bookings directly in Mongo and clean them up in fixture teardown. Also creates and hard-deletes a TEST_Service via admin CRUD endpoints.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All backend endpoints are functional. Reusable pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py runs in ~3s and can serve as regression tests. Customer OAuth session endpoint /api/auth/session was NOT hit (requires real Emergent session_id); it is covered indirectly by seeding sessions and validating /api/auth/me, /api/bookings, PDF invoice. Frontend testing was out of scope for this iteration.
